Musical artist

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/74/Tony-Scherr_06N6855.jpg/250px-Tony-Scherr_06N6855.jpg)](/wiki/File:Tony-Scherr_06N6855.jpg)

Tony Scherr, [moers festival](/wiki/Moers_festival "Moers festival") 2011

**Tony Scherr** is an American [jazz](/wiki/Jazz "Jazz") and [folk rock](/wiki/Folk_rock "Folk rock") bassist, guitarist, singer-songwriter, and record producer.

## Biography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Tony_Scherr&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Biography")]

Scherr was born in [New Haven, Connecticut](/wiki/New_Haven,_Connecticut "New Haven, Connecticut"), and is a graduate of the [Hammonasset School](/wiki/Hammonasset_School "Hammonasset School"). He played with [Woody Herman](/wiki/Woody_Herman "Woody Herman") as a teenager,[[1]](#cite_note-am-1) and moved to [New York City](/wiki/New_York_City "New York City") in the late-1980s, where he became a prolific session musician, working with artists such as [Bill Frisell](/wiki/Bill_Frisell "Bill Frisell"), [John Scofield](/wiki/John_Scofield "John Scofield"), [Norah Jones](/wiki/Norah_Jones "Norah Jones"), and [Ana Egge](/wiki/Ana_Egge "Ana Egge"). He has been a member of a number of bands, such as [The Lounge Lizards](/wiki/The_Lounge_Lizards "The Lounge Lizards"), [Sex Mob](/wiki/Sex_Mob_(band) "Sex Mob (band)"), [Jesse Harris and the Ferdinandos](/wiki/Jesse_Harris "Jesse Harris"), and [Chris Brown and the Citizen Band](/wiki/Chris_Brown_(Canadian_singer) "Chris Brown (Canadian singer)"). Scherr owns a recording studio[[1]](#cite_note-am-1) and has worked as record producer for many of the artists he performs with.

In 2002, Tony Scherr released his first solo album, *Come Around*, on [Smells Like Records](/wiki/Smells_Like_Records "Smells Like Records"). His song "Sacramento" was later covered by [Leslie Feist](/wiki/Leslie_Feist "Leslie Feist") on her album *[Let It Die](/wiki/Let_It_Die_(album) "Let It Die (album)")*, with alternate lyrics and a new title, "Lonely Lonely".

Tony Scherr's second album, *Twist in the Wind,* was released March 17, 2008.
